So, in code:

We create a newGrid as a copy of the original grid. Then, for each row in the submatrix, we extract the submatrix columns. Then, reverse the array of these subrows, and then put them back into the grid's rows, in the same columns.

So, in TypeScript code:

function solve(grid: number[][], x: number, y: number, k: number): number[][] {

    // Create a copy of the grid

    const newGrid = grid.map(row => [...row]);

    // Collect the submatrix rows

    const subRows = [];

    for (let i = 0; i < k; i++) {

        const row = newGrid[x + i];

        const sub = row.slice(y, y + k);

        subRows.push(sub);

    }

    // Reverse the subRows

    subRows.reverse();

    // Put them back into the grid

    for (let i = 0; i < k; i++) {

        const row = newGrid[x + i];

        for (let j = 0; j < k; j++) {

            row[y + j] = subRows[i][j];

        }

    }

    return newGrid;

}
